conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child – my son Alex. He's 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask you a few more questions about Alex to determine your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, attending school, college, or an approved training programme)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's currently in sixth form doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Has Alex started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or is he claiming any benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of those. He's just focused on his studies at the moment – he doesn't have a job and he's not claiming any benefits himself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the rules against your circumstances:

- Alex is 17 years old and in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels)
- He lives with you at home
- You both live in the UK (Bristol)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He is not working 24+ hours per week
- He is not claiming benefits in his own right

According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 17, living with you, and is in approved sixth form education doing his A-levels, you meet all the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since Alex is 17, living with you, and in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels), you meet all the eligibility requirements. Child Benefit can continue for young people under 20 who remain in approved education or training.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit and either keep the payments and pay any tax charge, or choose not to receive the payments but still get National Insurance credits.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any questions about the application process, please contact the Child Benefit Office.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant is responsible for Alex as he lives with them at home in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex has not started paid work for 24 hours or more per week, and is not claiming benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, is in approved education (sixth form), no one else is claiming for him, and he has not started full-time work or claimed benefits in his own right. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

The claimant is responsible for Alex as he lives with them at home in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, is in approved education (sixth form), no one else is claiming for him, and he has not started full-time work or claimed benefits in his own right. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
